subject: Re: Input movie format
body:
I've finally solved this - the problem was the K80 GPUs on AWS (for some 
reason). The g3 instances with m60 GPUs work fine, but not the K80 machines 
(p2). 

Mike

On Thursday, July 12, 2018 at 1:13:17 AM UTC-4, mcia...@umich.edu wrote:
>
> As a follow up - this appears to be an issue with my install of Warp. I've 
> installed Warp on AWS Windows Server 2016 on which I had installed CUDA9.2 
> and the latest Visual C++. 
>
> The problem is that no matter movie format I provide, .mrcs, .mrc, .tif, 
> Warp will run and then output a value of 0 for defocus, and the display 
> image will be black. While it runs, I can see that the GPU is doing 
> something as the amount of GPU RAM changes during the run. 
>
> Do you have any suggestions on how to troubleshoot this? I don't see any 
> log files or information anywhere. 
>
> If you'd prefer, I can set up a machine then give you remote desktop 
> access so you can look around. 
>
> Mike  
>
> On Wednesday, July 11, 2018 at 4:03:06 PM UTC-4, mcia...@umich.edu wrote:
>>
>> Hi Dmitry,
>>
>> We collect our movies using Leginon, and the movies are saved un-gain 
>> normalized in MRC format in Map Mode 6 (unsigned 16-bit integer). 
>>
>> I know that 1) these movies are not read by Warp and 2) if I convert 
>> these into TIFF using mrc2tif they are also not read by Warp. 
>>
>> What is the best way to store these as compressed files that are also 
>> readable by Warp?
>>
>> Thanks,
>> Mike
>>
>
